# Third-Party Licenses

`baldur-framework` (Apache-2.0) depends on the open-source packages listed below. They are installed separately from PyPI — not vendored into the baldur wheel — so this inventory is provided for attribution and license-compliance transparency, not as a redistribution of their code.

Scope: the resolved `baldur-framework[all]` dependency closure (development and test tooling is excluded). Versions reflect the environment the inventory was generated in; regenerate at release time.

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/generate_notices.py — do not edit by hand. -->

Regenerate with:

```
pip install -e ".[all]"
python scripts/generate_notices.py
```

## Copyleft dependencies

No strong copyleft (GPL / AGPL) licenses are present in the dependency tree.

The following use weak/library copyleft (LGPL). baldur consumes them as separately pip-installed packages (dynamic linking), which imposes no source-disclosure obligation on baldur's own code. If you redistribute a *bundled* artifact (e.g. a Docker image or single-file build) that embeds these binaries, include their license text and note source availability:

| Package | Version | License | Source |
|---------|---------|---------|--------|
| crontab | 1.0.5 | GNU Library or Lesser General Public License (LGPL); GNU Lesser General Public License v2 (LGPLv2); GNU Lesser General Public License v3 (LGPLv3) | https://github.com/josiahcarlson/parse-crontab |
| psycopg2-binary | 2.9.11 | GNU Library or Lesser General Public License (LGPL) | https://psycopg.org/ |
